title: Green-space and soil-sealing monitoring for planning compliance
summary: A planning authority used CLMS Urban Atlas and imperviousness products to monitor green space and soil sealing against policy targets.
challenge: Densification was eroding green space and increasing soil sealing, but the authority lacked consistent, repeatable monitoring to track change and demonstrate compliance with EU and local targets.
approach: CLMS Urban Atlas, imperviousness and tree-cover products from Sentinel-2 provided harmonised, repeatable measurement of green and sealed surfaces across the region.
results: The authority gained a defensible evidence base for planning decisions and green-infrastructure strategy, and could report progress against soil-sealing and green-space targets.
